verses.text: A great multitude follows for His miracles, the passover near. Hungry crowds. They come, drawn by His works.
A great multitude follows for His miracles, the passover near. Hungry crowds. They come, drawn by His works.
A great multitude follows for His miracles, the passover near. Hungry crowds. They come, drawn by His works.
A great multitude follows for His miracles, the passover near. Hungry crowds. They come, drawn by His works.
Whence buy bread? a lad has five loaves and two fishes; 'what are they among so many?' Small means. The need dwarfs the supply in their eyes.
Whence buy bread? a lad has five loaves and two fishes; 'what are they among so many?' Small means. The need dwarfs the supply in their eyes.
Whence buy bread? a lad has five loaves and two fishes; 'what are they among so many?' Small means. The need dwarfs the supply in their eyes.
Whence buy bread? a lad has five loaves and two fishes; 'what are they among so many?' Small means. The need dwarfs the supply in their eyes.
Whence buy bread? a lad has five loaves and two fishes; 'what are they among so many?' Small means. The need dwarfs the supply in their eyes.
He gives thanks, and all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little feeds the multitude.
He gives thanks, and all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little feeds the multitude.
He gives thanks, and all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little feeds the multitude.
He gives thanks, and all five thousand are filled, twelve baskets over. Bountiful supply. In His hands the little feeds the multitude.
They would take Him by force to make Him king; He withdraws alone. Crown refused. He will not be the king they imagine.
They would take Him by force to make Him king; He withdraws alone. Crown refused. He will not be the king they imagine.
At evening a storm rises as the disciples cross. Toil and fear. They labour against the wind without their Lord.
At evening a storm rises as the disciples cross. Toil and fear. They labour against the wind without their Lord.
At evening a storm rises as the disciples cross. Toil and fear. They labour against the wind without their Lord.
He comes walking on the sea, 'It is I; be not afraid'; at once they reach land. Present help. He comes to His own in the storm.
He comes walking on the sea, 'It is I; be not afraid'; at once they reach land. Present help. He comes to His own in the storm.
He comes walking on the sea, 'It is I; be not afraid'; at once they reach land. Present help. He comes to His own in the storm.
The crowd seeks Him across the sea. Eager but earthly. They want the bread, not the Bread-giver.
The crowd seeks Him across the sea. Eager but earthly. They want the bread, not the Bread-giver.
The crowd seeks Him across the sea. Eager but earthly. They want the bread, not the Bread-giver.
Ye seek Me for the loaves; 'labour... for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life.' Higher hunger. He turns them from food that perishes to life.
Ye seek Me for the loaves; 'labour... for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life.' Higher hunger. He turns them from food that perishes to life.
Ye seek Me for the loaves; 'labour... for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life.' Higher hunger. He turns them from food that perishes to life.
'This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.' Faith first. The great work God asks is to trust His Son.
'This is the work of God, that ye believe on him whom he hath sent.' Faith first. The great work God asks is to trust His Son.
The bread of God comes down from heaven and 'giveth life unto the world.' True bread. Christ is the heaven-sent nourishment of souls.
The bread of God comes down from heaven and 'giveth life unto the world.' True bread. Christ is the heaven-sent nourishment of souls.
The bread of God comes down from heaven and 'giveth life unto the world.' True bread. Christ is the heaven-sent nourishment of souls.
The bread of God comes down from heaven and 'giveth life unto the world.' True bread. Christ is the heaven-sent nourishment of souls.
'I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.' Full satisfaction. Christ alone fills the hungry soul.
'I am the bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never thirst.' Full satisfaction. Christ alone fills the hungry soul.
All the Father gives Me shall come, and I will lose none, but raise them at the last day. Kept secure. Those given to Christ are safe to the end.
All the Father gives Me shall come, and I will lose none, but raise them at the last day. Kept secure. Those given to Christ are safe to the end.
All the Father gives Me shall come, and I will lose none, but raise them at the last day. Kept secure. Those given to Christ are safe to the end.
All the Father gives Me shall come, and I will lose none, but raise them at the last day. Kept secure. Those given to Christ are safe to the end.
All the Father gives Me shall come, and I will lose none, but raise them at the last day. Kept secure. Those given to Christ are safe to the end.
The Jews murmur; 'No man can come to me, except the Father... draw him.' Drawn by grace. Coming to Christ is the Father's work in us.
The Jews murmur; 'No man can come to me, except the Father... draw him.' Drawn by grace. Coming to Christ is the Father's work in us.
The Jews murmur; 'No man can come to me, except the Father... draw him.' Drawn by grace. Coming to Christ is the Father's work in us.
The Jews murmur; 'No man can come to me, except the Father... draw him.' Drawn by grace. Coming to Christ is the Father's work in us.
Every one taught of God comes to Me; 'he that believeth on me hath everlasting life.' Taught and trusting. The drawn soul believes and lives.
Every one taught of God comes to Me; 'he that believeth on me hath everlasting life.' Taught and trusting. The drawn soul believes and lives.
Every one taught of God comes to Me; 'he that believeth on me hath everlasting life.' Taught and trusting. The drawn soul believes and lives.
Every one taught of God comes to Me; 'he that believeth on me hath everlasting life.' Taught and trusting. The drawn soul believes and lives.
'I am the living bread'; if any eat of it he lives for ever; the bread is My flesh. Life by His flesh. Christ gives Himself for the world's life.
'I am the living bread'; if any eat of it he lives for ever; the bread is My flesh. Life by His flesh. Christ gives Himself for the world's life.
'I am the living bread'; if any eat of it he lives for ever; the bread is My flesh. Life by His flesh. Christ gives Himself for the world's life.
Except ye eat His flesh and drink His blood, ye have no life; 'my flesh is meat indeed.' Feeding on Christ. Faith feeds on the crucified Saviour.
Except ye eat His flesh and drink His blood, ye have no life; 'my flesh is meat indeed.' Feeding on Christ. Faith feeds on the crucified Saviour.
Except ye eat His flesh and drink His blood, ye have no life; 'my flesh is meat indeed.' Feeding on Christ. Faith feeds on the crucified Saviour.
Except ye eat His flesh and drink His blood, ye have no life; 'my flesh is meat indeed.' Feeding on Christ. Faith feeds on the crucified Saviour.
He that eats My flesh 'dwelleth in me'; he shall live by Me. Mutual abiding. To feed on Christ is to live in union with Him.
He that eats My flesh 'dwelleth in me'; he shall live by Me. Mutual abiding. To feed on Christ is to live in union with Him.
He that eats My flesh 'dwelleth in me'; he shall live by Me. Mutual abiding. To feed on Christ is to live in union with Him.
He that eats My flesh 'dwelleth in me'; he shall live by Me. Mutual abiding. To feed on Christ is to live in union with Him.
Many say, 'This is an hard saying'; what if ye see the Son of man ascend? Stumbled. The carnal mind cannot grasp the spiritual word.
Many say, 'This is an hard saying'; what if ye see the Son of man ascend? Stumbled. The carnal mind cannot grasp the spiritual word.
Many say, 'This is an hard saying'; what if ye see the Son of man ascend? Stumbled. The carnal mind cannot grasp the spiritual word.
'The words that I speak... are spirit, and... life'; none can come but as given of the Father. Spirit and life. His words quicken the believing heart.
'The words that I speak... are spirit, and... life'; none can come but as given of the Father. Spirit and life. His words quicken the believing heart.
'The words that I speak... are spirit, and... life'; none can come but as given of the Father. Spirit and life. His words quicken the believing heart.
Many go back; 'Will ye also go away?' 'Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.' Steadfast faith. Peter clings to Christ when others fall away.
Many go back; 'Will ye also go away?' 'Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.' Steadfast faith. Peter clings to Christ when others fall away.
Many go back; 'Will ye also go away?' 'Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.' Steadfast faith. Peter clings to Christ when others fall away.
Many go back; 'Will ye also go away?' 'Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the words of eternal life.' Steadfast faith. Peter clings to Christ when others fall away.
'Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?' He spoke of Judas. A hidden traitor. Even the chosen band held a false heart.
'Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?' He spoke of Judas. A hidden traitor. Even the chosen band held a false heart.
